VINIT.justification: The initial state before the introduction of non-Hermitian parameters.

TANCH.justification: The boundary of the parameter space where the eigenvalue spectrum is fully defined.

AFWD.justification: The directed movement through the complex parameter space toward the singularity.

AREV.justification: The reverse flow of energy or information out of the system into the environment.

CLINK.justification: The sequential application of perturbations to the system matrix.

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity of the operator where the output state maps back to the input basis.

FSPLIT.justification: The splitting of the eigenvalue trajectory into distinct topological branches.

FFUSE.justification: The merging of distinct eigenvectors into a single degenerate state at the exceptional point.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative branch where the system maintains a bounded response.

EVALF.justification: The negative branch where the system exhibits runaway or divergent behavior.

ENGAGR.justification: The state where T and F branches are indistinguishable and simultaneously occupied.

IFIX.justification: The permanent Berry phase shift recorded after a complete circuit around the singularity.

reach the exceptional point where sheets intersect and modes are held simultaneously
coalesce the eigenvectors into a single degenerate state
record the permanent topological winding number of the encounter
seal the spectral manifold within the defined parameter boundary
